title: PHP Functions
content:
### PHP Functions

A function is a block of reusable code that performs a specific task.

### Defining a Function

```php
<?php
function functionName() {
    // code to execute
}
?>
```

### Example

```php
<?php
function sayHello() {
    echo "Hello, World!";
}

sayHello(); // Call the function
?>
```

### Function Naming Rules

| Rule | Details |
|------|---------|
| Start with letter or underscore | Must not start with a number |
| Case-insensitive | But should be called as defined |
| Unique names | No two functions with the same name |
| Descriptive | Use meaningful names (camelCase or snake_case) |

### Types of Functions

| Type | Description |
|------|-------------|
| User-defined | Created by the developer |
| Built-in | Provided by PHP (e.g., `strlen()`, `array_merge()`) |
| Anonymous | Functions without a name (closures) |
| Arrow (PHP 7.4+) | Shorthand anonymous functions |

> **Key Point:** Functions must be defined before they are called, but PHP allows calling a function before its definition in the same file.
codeExample:
<?php
// Simple function
function greet() {
    return "Welcome!";
}

// Call it
echo greet();

// Function with logic
function isEven($num) {
    return $num % 2 == 0;
}

if (isEven(10)) {
    echo "10 is even";
}
?>

quiz.question: What keyword is used to define a function in PHP?
Can a function be called before its definition?
